videos.title: Comparing and Ordering Numbers up to 5 Digits
videos.description: Learn to compare 4 and 5 digit numbers using greater than, less than, and equal to signs. Also practice arranging numbers in ascending and descending order.
learningObjectives: Use place value to determine which number is greater
Arrange numbers in ascending and descending order
keyConceptsCovered: Comparing by number of digits first
Comparing digit by digit from the left

videos.title: Place Value up to 5 Digits | Ten Thousands Place
videos.description: Learn about place value in 5-digit numbers. Understand ones, tens, hundreds, thousands, and ten thousands places with real-life examples like distances and populations!
learningObjectives: Understand why the leftmost digit is most important for comparing
keyConceptsCovered: Using place value to compare numbers

videos.title: Comparing 3-Digit Numbers
videos.description: Learn to compare 3-digit numbers using greater than, less than, and equal to. Start from the hundreds place and work your way down to compare numbers correctly.
learningObjectives: Apply the same method to larger numbers
keyConceptsCovered: Compare starting from the highest place

videos.title: Place Value Practice | All Forms of 5-Digit Numbers
videos.description: Practice writing 5-digit numbers in standard form, expanded form, and word form. Strengthen your understanding of place value with plenty of examples and exercises.
learningObjectives: Practice number forms to support comparison skills
Strengthen understanding of digit values for accurate comparison
keyConceptsCovered: Connecting place value understanding to comparison

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Comparing and place value review for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Start with 3-digit comparison review, build up to 5-digit

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites
after_prerequisite_review.rationale: Essential comparing and ordering concepts only

questions.question: Arrange in descending order: 45,230 ; 54,320 ; 43,520
questions.explanation: Descending means largest to smallest. 54,320 is the largest (5 ten thousands), then 45,230 (4 ten thousands, 5 thousands), then 43,520 (4 ten thousands, 3 thousands).

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use a place value comparison chart. Write two numbers in columns and compare digit by digit from the left. Have students use > and < symbols and explain their reasoning.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Compare prices, distances, or populations with your child. Ask 'Which city has more people?' or 'Which item costs more?' and discuss how to compare the numbers.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: When comparing big numbers, always start from the left! Check the biggest place value first. If those digits are the same, move to the next place.
